Workflow role: Defines biologically plausible cell states and pathway targets before any imaging linkage is attempted
LEMPA executed source/status: Human Cell Atlas lung atlas; 584944 cells across 50 annotated cell types; strong atlas layer retained
Minimum requirement for future studies: Organ-matched reference with stable cell annotation and clearly defined pathway programs
Common failure if weak or absent: Targets may be biologically implausible or too noisy to justify downstream validation
LEMPA lesson: A strong atlas can be publishable and reusable even when patient-level radiogenomic validation fails

Data layer: Paired bulk RNA-seq or transcriptomic target layer
Workflow role: Supplies patient-level molecular targets for deconvolution module construction and pathway scoring
LEMPA executed source/status: Paired NSCLC transcriptomic layer available for 130 linked patients after inner join
Minimum requirement for future studies: One-to-one patient-linked molecular matrix with QC and sample manifest
Common failure if weak or absent: Imaging cannot be benchmarked against patient-level molecular truth
LEMPA lesson: Public paired RNA is the core bridge layer; without it radiogenomics remains speculative

Workflow role: Provides the non-invasive predictor space to be tested against transcriptomic targets
LEMPA executed source/status: 162 radiomics-processing patients; 130 retained in paired analysis; QC feature table exists
Minimum requirement for future studies: Harmonized feature extraction modality provenance and explicit QC thresholds
Common failure if weak or absent: Feature instability or untracked preprocessing can overwhelm any biologic signal
LEMPA lesson: Imaging availability alone did not guarantee recoverable atlas-derived targets

Workflow role: Defines what tissue is measured and whether radiomic predictors are reproducible
LEMPA executed source/status: Mask provenance available in frozen outputs; H4b audit showed SEG=117 and fallback_body_outline=12
Minimum requirement for future studies: Auditable mask source for each patient and clear fallback rules
Common failure if weak or absent: Feature values may drift because the sampled region is inconsistent or weakly documented
LEMPA lesson: Mask provenance should be surfaced explicitly because it affects trust in feature-based associations

Workflow role: Enables attrition accounting held-out robustness checks and descriptive confounding review
LEMPA executed source/status: Pairing attrition documented; site/vendor metadata available but too limited for robust V2 held-out splits
Minimum requirement for future studies: Site scanner batch histology and linkage metadata with enough spread to support robustness testing
Common failure if weak or absent: Transportability cannot be assessed; apparent internal findings may be non-generalizable
LEMPA lesson: V2-G5 could not be estimated because frozen local strata were not rich enough for held-out robustness

Workflow role: Converts an internal stress test into a real replication surface
LEMPA executed source/status: Not available; Phase C remains dormant pending explicit trigger criteria
Minimum requirement for future studies: Independent paired imaging-plus-RNA cohort with N >= 100 one-to-one linkage and technical covariates
Common failure if weak or absent: The study can report only an internal benchmark or negative stress test not external validation
LEMPA lesson: No external paired cohort means archive the broad line rather than narrate a near-success
